lastReleaseMessage: Restore dates.ts after a botched regex replacement in the...

barRaise.topFinding: Fourteenth report, eighth day with HEAD frozen at 890b493 (2026-06-13); the working tree is byte-identical to the 06-19 and 06-20 passes (same four modified files, two untracked .bak, two tracked .bak, uncommitted git-guard.ps1). Today's canonical audit ran clean again, zero doc-vs-code drift. This run was triggered by hand, so the standing pause-the-cadence advice does not apply to it, but the scheduled bar-raise-scheduler-daily is still re-deriving an identical report. The single HIGH is unchanged: the ship gate has failed eight consecutive cycles. The sharpened nuance this cycle is that the fix is NOT a bare release.ps1 run; git add -A would sweep the untracked .bak files into HEAD while *.bak is still ungitignored. The clean ship is gitignore-*.bak, git rm the two tracked .bak, delete the two untracked, then release. Three small steps and the verdict reverts to healthy.

actions.title: Ship the tree in the right order so the release does not self-pollute: add *.bak to .gitignore, git rm --cached the two tracked .bak files, delete the two untracked .bak, then cd X:\YesAndScheduler; .\scripts\release.ps1. Closes the reliability HIGH plus the maintainability/audit-loop/ps1-stack MEDIUMs and the observability/version-pill cross-references. origin/main...HEAD = 0 0, nothing external blocks it.

Pause bar-raise-scheduler-daily (or drop to twice-weekly) in the task registry until HEAD moves. The daily run is re-deriving a byte-identical report; manual bar-raise YaS runs stay available on demand.
Rename package.json:5 description to 'Yes& Scheduler' without the U+2026 ellipsis and U+2014 em-dash, in the ship commit.
